skill.name: remotion-render
skill.description: Render React and Remotion TSX components into MP4 videos through the inference.sh CLI.
skill.summary: Turn Remotion component code into configurable MP4 renders.
skill.author: tul-sh

security_audit.summary: The external command findings are mostly documentation examples for the allowed inference.sh CLI, not hidden executable code. The meaningful risk is that the skill sends user-provided TSX to a remote rendering service, so users should avoid secrets and untrusted code.

medium_findings.title: Remote Rendering of User-Supplied TSX
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to pass React component TSX as the code input to a remote infsh/remotion-render app. This is expected behavior, but it can expose proprietary code or secrets if users include them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly shows TSX code being sent to the inference.sh app. This is legitimate for the skill, but it creates clear privacy and remote execution exposure.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill depends on the infsh CLI and provides commands that run remote apps. The command examples are not covert execution, but users should understand that rendering occurs outside the local workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible user instructions and match the skill purpose. Risk remains medium because they require an external CLI and remote service interaction.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External Documentation and Asset URLs
low_findings.description: The skill links to inference.sh, Remotion documentation, and a hosted image. These are disclosed documentation links and do not show evidence of data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are plainly visible documentation and image references. No hidden request or credential transfer is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high static findings map to ordinary descriptive text and a table header, not cryptographic code. No evidence found of weak hash or cipher usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain frontmatter description text and the word Description in a Markdown table. They do not contain cryptographic operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Code Submission Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The render workflow sends component source text as input to a remote app. This is intentional, but it should be treated as remote code processing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Multiple examples show a code field submitted to infsh/remotion-render. The pattern is semantically confirmed by the skill name and instructions.

content.value_statement: Teams need repeatable video renders without building a full render pipeline. This skill turns Remotion component code into MP4 outputs through inference.sh.
actual_capabilities: Renders default-exported React and Remotion components.
Accepts duration, frame rate, width, height, and codec settings.
Supports common Remotion APIs such as spring and interpolate.
Passes component props into the rendered composition.
Shows CLI and Python SDK workflows for rendering.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI or SDK to be installed and authenticated.
Sends component code and props to a remote rendering service.
Does not provide local sandboxing or source code validation.
Only documents supported imports from React and Remotion.

use_cases.title: Automated Marketing Clips
use_cases.description: Create short branded animations from reusable Remotion components.

use_cases.title: Data-Driven Video Reports
use_cases.description: Render charts, counters, and summaries from generated component props.

use_cases.title: Prototype Motion Concepts
use_cases.description: Convert React animation ideas into shareable MP4 files quickly.

prompt_templates.title: Render Basic Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple Remotion component for a three-second fade-in title video. Use 1920 by 1080 and 30 fps.

prompt_templates.title: Add Timed Sequences
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Remotion video with three timed text sequences. Include duration, fps, and render settings for inference.sh.

prompt_templates.title: Use Dynamic Props
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Remotion component that accepts title, subtitle, and colors as props for repeated video renders.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Render Specification
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Remotion render request for missing settings, privacy risks, and compatibility with the documented inference.sh renderer.

output_examples.input: A request for a short welcome animation with a dark background.
output_examples.output: A render plan with component structure, duration, frame rate, dimensions, and expected MP4 output.

output_examples.input: A product metrics video that uses supplied title and percentage values.
output_examples.output: A prop-driven Remotion approach with animation timing and remote render settings.

output_examples.input: A request to convert a React animation idea into a video deliverable.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation outline and guidance for submitting it to the inference.sh renderer.

best_practices: Keep TSX inputs small and focused on the final composition.
Remove secrets, private URLs, and proprietary data before rendering.
Specify duration, fps, width, and height for predictable output.
anti_patterns: Do not submit untrusted third-party TSX without review.
Do not include credentials, tokens, or customer data in props.
Do not rely on undocumented imports or browser-only APIs.

faq.question: What does this skill render?
faq.answer: It renders default-exported React and Remotion components into MP4 video files.

faq.question: Does it render locally?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflow uses inference.sh through the infsh CLI or Python SDK.

faq.question: Can I configure video size?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented inputs include width, height, fps, duration, and codec.

faq.question: Can I pass dynamic values?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents a props object for component inputs.

faq.question: Is it safe for private code?
faq.answer: Use caution. The workflow sends component code to a remote service for rendering.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
